top_10.rationale: Original 'Attention Is All You Need' paper (Vaswani et al.) — the canonical primary source defining the transformer and its attention mechanism.
Jay Alammar's Illustrated Transformer — the most widely referenced visual walk-through of the attention mechanism; authoritative community standard for understanding.
Lilian Weng's 'Attention? Attention' blog post — canonical, detailed survey of attention mechanisms from Bahdanau through self-attention, highly cited practitioner reference.
Dive into Deep Learning textbook chapter on attention and transformers — comprehensive interactive textbook with code, visualizations, and mathematical grounding.
3Blue1Brown step-by-step attention lesson — outstanding geometric/visual explanation of scaled dot-product attention, highly accessible and accurate.
Machine Learning Mastery guide to transformer attention — practical implementation-focused explanation with code for scaled dot-product and multi-head attention.
Wikipedia article on the Transformer — solid overview of the architecture including scaled dot-product attention formulation and architectural context.
Survey 'Understanding Transformers and Attention Mechanisms' — recent comprehensive academic survey, title directly matches query intent.
Tutorial and survey covering attention mechanism, transformers, BERT, and GPT — broad scope maps well to someone seeking a structured overview.
Complete guide to transformer architecture and attention — covers theory through implementation including multi-head attention and positional encoding in one comprehensive post.
